title: Apex Ventures Doubles Down on Quantum with Volta Bet
body:
For Apex Ventures, the $400 million check written to Volta Systems last week represents more than a financial bet — it signals the firm's strategic shift toward what managing partner Diana Chu calls "deep infrastructure."

Apex, best known for consumer software plays in the early 2010s, has quietly repositioned itself over the past three years. Its portfolio now includes two climate-tech companies, a defense AI firm, and now Volta, which is building what it describes as fault-tolerant quantum processors.

"We are done chasing app-layer returns," Chu told Venture Desk in an interview. "The infrastructure layer is where the durable value is."

The Volta deal is Apex's largest single check to date. Sources familiar with the negotiations said Apex initially sought a co-lead with a strategic investor but ultimately moved forward alone after timing concerns arose.

Volta's new CTO, E. Vasquez — who joins from IBM Research — was reportedly a factor in Apex's final decision. The firm had previously passed on an earlier Volta round in 2024, a decision Chu acknowledged was "a miss we're correcting."
